probe_method: Sequential load of all 4 models on 8GB VRAM. Observe eviction order. Attempt GLM (8GB) load. Document what evicts and what fails.

probes.result: 3 models unloaded via keep_alive:0. 1 model still showed in /api/ps after 5s (Nemotron slow unload).

probes.note: Qwen (6.43GB) + Llama (2.75GB) = 9.18GB exceeds available VRAM (~4.8GB after Ollama base). Qwen evicted as expected (LRU — oldest unused).

probes.result: HTTP 500: llama runner process has terminated
probes.note: GLM crashes regardless of VRAM state. Tested with empty VRAM — same crash. GLM model file is 8GB but effective VRAM needed exceeds available ~4.8GB (8192 - 3362 Ollama base).

probes.step: GLM on fully empty VRAM (all models unloaded first)
probes.result: HTTP 500: llama runner process has terminated
probes.note: Confirmed: GLM failure is not VRAM contention — it fails even with 0 models loaded. Root cause: Ollama base process consumes 3362 MiB, leaving only ~4.8GB for model. GLM at 8GB cannot fit.

eviction_behavior.observed_pattern: LRU-consistent for functional models. When new model requires more VRAM than available, oldest-unused model evicts first.

evidence_summary: Eviction is LRU-predictable for the 3 loadable models (Qwen, Llama, Nemotron). Only small-model pairs (Llama 2.75GB + Nemotron 3.05GB = 5.8GB) can coexist on 8GB VRAM. GLM (8GB) cannot load at all — the Ollama process base overhead (3362 MiB) leaves insufficient VRAM. This is a regression from W0 where GLM was functional.
workaround: GLM cannot participate in any VRAM-bound operation on 8GB hardware. Cascade must use 3 models max. GLM usable only with CPU offloading if Ollama supports it, or on higher-VRAM hardware.
shippability_impact: 4-model cascade is not achievable on 8GB VRAM with current Ollama. 3-model cascade (Qwen, Llama, Nemotron) is viable.
